reasoning: US State Department travel advisory for Mozambique is Level 3 as of December 20, 2024 - confirmed by authoritative US Embassy source. The April 21, 2025 downgrade to Level 2 mentioned in some search results appears to have been temporary or incorrect, as the Embassy's official Level 3 advisory from December remains active. Despite 400+ deaths in post-election protests (Oct 2024-Mar 2025) and serious civil unrest, the US assessment has NOT elevated to Level 4 even during the peak crisis. Level 4 requires catastrophic conditions (terrorism, kidnapping, state of emergency) seen in Burkina Faso/CAR. Northern Mozambique has had ISIS insurgency for years without triggering Level 4 country-wide. Two-month window to July 1, 2025 is insufficient for escalation from current Level 3 to Level 4. Additionally, the inauguration of President Chapo in January 2025 and subsequent crackdown have not led to Level 4 despite 300+ deaths. The trend toward stabilization is evidenced by specific regional Level 4 designations in northern provinces while maintaining Level 3 nationally.
